name: Arguis Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Arguis — circular por Nueno hasta la sierra de Gratal
description: This difficult 23.3-mile touring cycling route around Embalse de Arguis offers challenging climbs and stunning views of the Sierra de Gratal
region_intro: Touring cycling around Nueno offers diverse landscapes in the pre-Pyrenean region of Huesca, Aragon. The area features rugged mountains and deep canyons of the Sierra de Guara, alongside the scenic Embalse de Arguis. River landscapes provide gentler terrain, while other routes present challenging climbs and varied surfaces, including both paved and unpaved sections. This mix of terrain caters to different cycling preferences and skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Nueno The most popular touring cycling route is Arguis Reservoir Loop, a 23.3 miles (37.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers challenging climbs and views of the Sierra de Gratal. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Nocito Village – Dolmen of Ibirque loop from Nocito, a difficult 20.2 miles (32.5 km) path. This route explores the rugged terrain around Nocito village, featuring significant elevation gain. Local touring cyclists also love the Salto Roldán Viewpoint – Santa Eulalia de la Peña loop from Nueno, a 10.7 miles (17.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Nueno is defined by rugged mountains, river landscapes, and the scenic Embalse de Arguis. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult climbs. The routes in Nueno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nueno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Nueno?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;14 touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Nueno, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Nueno?
A: Nueno's touring cycling routes offer a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find challenging climbs and rugged sections in the &lt;em&gt;Sierra de Guara&lt;/em&gt;, scenic paths around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and gentler, often paved, routes along river landscapes. This variety ensures options for both experienced cyclists and those seeking more relaxed rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Nueno?
A: The pre-Pyrenean region around Nueno is generally best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the canyons, while winter may bring colder conditions and potential snow at higher elevations, making some routes more challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Nueno?
A: While many routes in Nueno feature challenging climbs, there are &lt;strong&gt;5 moderate routes&lt;/strong&gt; available. These routes offer a good balance for those looking for a less strenuous ride compared to the more difficult options, often following river landscapes or traditional paths.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Nueno?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Nueno are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974800400/arguis-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31859402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto Roldán Viewpoint – Santa Eulalia de la Peña loop from Nueno&lt;/a&gt; are excellent circular options.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in Nueno?
A: The routes often pass through stunning natural landscapes. You can cycle around the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The rugged canyons of the &lt;em&gt;Sierra de Guara&lt;/em&gt; are also a prominent feature, with highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5040942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgas de San Julián&lt;/a&gt; offering impressive views.

Q: Are there any historical sites or villages to explore along the routes?
A: Yes, touring cycling routes in the Nueno area often pass through charming historic villages, providing cultural stops. You can also discover ancient sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2984930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Losa Mora&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Ibirque&lt;/a&gt;, which add an element of historical intrigue to your ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists appreciate about the routes in Nueno?
A: The routes in Nueno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain terrain to serene river routes, and the immersive experience of connecting with nature and local culture in this quiet region.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Nueno?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, the region's varied terrain includes gentler river landscapes that can be suitable for families. Look for routes described as 'moderate' difficulty, which may offer more manageable distances and less challenging climbs for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Nueno?
A: Many natural areas in Spain, including those around Nueno, are generally dog-friendly, especially on trails. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural parks like the &lt;em&gt;Sierra de Guara&lt;/em&gt;, to protect wildlife and ensure their safety. Check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: Where can I find amenities like cafes or accommodation along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes in Nueno often pass through charming historic villages. These villages are good places to find local cafes, restaurants, and sometimes small accommodations. Planning your route to include stops in these villages will ensure you have access to amenities.

Q: What are some of the most challenging touring cycling routes in Nueno?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10466308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nocito Village – Dolmen of Ibirque loop from Nocito&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and rugged terrain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31511173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila – Flumen Terraces loop from Santa María de Belsué&lt;/a&gt;, known for its substantial climbs.

Q: Is public transport or parking available near the cycling routes in Nueno?
A: Nueno is a municipality in the province of Huesca, and while public transport options might be limited directly to trailheads, parking is generally available in and around the main villages. It's recommended to check specific starting points for designated parking areas before your ride.
tips: Igriés is a town in the Foia d'Osca region, right in front of the Aragonese Pre-Pyrenees and from where you can see the Sierra de Gratal and the western part of the Guara mountain range. It is located just below the towering ridges of Santacoba peak, more than 770 meters high.

Alternative path to the passageway that goes from Nueno to the Arguis reservoir. In the past, this corridor between mountains was the "Cañada Real de Huesca a Monrepós", a place of passage from the capital to towns in the north of the province.

A little more than 20 kilometers from Huesca, behind the Gratal and El Águila mountains, is Arguis. A picturesque town that recalls the Pyrenean villages for its stone houses topped by the typical scarecrows from their chimneys. It also has a beautiful Romanesque-style church dating from the 12th century.

Surrounded by a beautiful landscape, its reservoir appears, built at the beginning of the 18th century, it is a must for fans of fishing and hiking, since there are many routes that run through these places.

Next to the reservoir, in the old forest house, the Arguis Interpretation Center opens its doors for you to get to know in depth, and in a dynamic and interactive way, the peculiarities of the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park.

More information: https://www.turismodearagon.com/ficha/arguis/

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for the Embalse de Arguis circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a "hard bike ride" and requires very good fitness. It involves significant elevation changes and some challenging segments, making it unsuitable for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 37.5 km long with an elevation gain of 670 meters. While mostly paved, some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride, potentially requiring you to push your bike. The surface breakdown includes 23.2 km of road, 11.0 km of path, and 3.08 km of street.

faqs.question: What scenic views or points of interest can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque landscapes typical of the Aragonese Pre-Pyrenees, with views of the Sierra de Gratal and the western part of the Guara mountain range. The Arguis Reservoir itself is beautifully situated. From the 1,563-meter summit of Pico Gratal, you can see the Sierra de Guara, the Hoya de Huesca, the Embalse de la Sotonera, the city of Huesca, and the Peña del Salto de Roldán on a clear day. You'll also pass through varied landscapes of moorland and coniferous forests, and the Isuela River Valley and Tunnel Road.

faqs.question: Are there any historical or cultural sites near the Arguis Reservoir?
faqs.answer: Yes, near the Arguis Reservoir, you can find a beautiful Romanesque-style church dating from the 12th century. Additionally, the Arguis Interpretation Center, located in the old forest house next to the reservoir, offers insights into the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Embalse de Arguis circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the route is circular and can be joined from various points, a common starting point is near the Arguis Reservoir or the nearby village of Nueno. Parking facilities are typically available in these areas, though it's always good to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is estimated to take approximately 3 hours, based on an average touring bicycle pace. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific entrance fees or permits required for cycling on established routes within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and be mindful of protected areas.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Arguis circular cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in natural areas, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and potential for other cyclists or hikers. Some sections might be challenging for dogs due to the terrain or if you need to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Embalse de Arguis circular route?
faqs.answer: The Aragonese Pre-Pyrenees can experience varied weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures or snow at higher elevations, especially towards the Sierra de Gratal.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the [CIMA HU10] Túnel de Manzanera * Nueno (21%) and passes through sections of the Gorgas de San Julián (19%), Camino de Igriés a Apiés (10%), and Camino de Lierta a Nueno (9%), among others.

faqs.question: Are there any places to stop for food or refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: The village of Igriés and Nueno, which are near or on the route, may offer amenities like cafes or small shops. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the more remote sections of the trail.

waypoints.name: Isuela River Valley and Tunnel Road
